findings.summary: #212 tests called engineDrift() directly and never inspected a DoctorReport — deleting the drift warning from the shipped tool (if(false)) left 30/30 green; added 4 runDoctor integration cases + an injectable DoctorOpts.engineCommit seam (ENGINE_COMMIT is the 'dev' sentinel under the test build, making the branch otherwise unreachable)
#188 plugin arm asserted only negatives through c?.detail ?? '' — gutting the message to a bare 'no manifest.json' left 15/15 green; added positive status+content assertions

suppressing the sentinel as drift made the real condition invisible while #212's stamping half stays open; compat detail now reports 'provenance unknown (#212)' — verified reaching the terminal through the real CLI, not just the API
isEngineWiring exported with no consumer and no direct test — now covered by 3 unit cases
dead optional backslash before the separator meant a quoted subcommand missed; predicate now un-escapes to literal command text, re-probed over 20 shapes + 6 malformed inputs
not.toBe('warn') could pass with the check absent — tightened to toBe('ok')
